name: Upcycling Hang, Weave and Remake
description:
Image Credits: Simi Mary
Turn your waste materials into something beautiful!

In this weaving workshop, participants learn the basics of weaving using different types of looms, including circular looms, frame looms, and branch weaving. Most materials are sourced from waste cloth, encouraging sustainability and creative reuse, while wool and threads are used to explore varied textures and patterns.

Participants are also invited to bring their own waste fabric to integrate into their work. 

This event is held in conjunction with our exhibition, mild tooth of milk, currently on display at the Dutch Warehouse in Fort Kochi.

name: Patchwork Cat Studio
description:
Create your own playful patchwork cat with artist Vasundhara W in this fun, hands-on workshop inspired by geometric shapes and fabric craft. Using tangram-style forms, colourful fabric, and simple stitching techniques, participants will design amusing cats in a variety of poses.

By the end of the session, you’ll have a fun patchwork cat placemat to take home.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Curator’s POV
description:
How has the most meme’able, memorable and mischievous animal known to man been represented across art history in the subcontinent?

Join curator Khushi Bansal as she explores the many lives of the Indian cat—appearing in paintings, sculptures, matchbox covers, artist sketchbooks, and beyond.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Make at MAP
description:
What happens when art meets memes? Come find out by turning artworks featuring cats into your own witty meme in this edition of Make at MAP.

Make @ MAP is a series that allows you to explore our collection in an engaging and hands-on manner. These sessions allow you to create and take home a special souvenir of your visit. It is perfect for all ages — so bring your friends and family and get ready to make some art! No experience is necessary, just a willingness to have fun and experiment with new materials.

name: If These Walls Could Speak
description:
Image Credits: Anoushka
Have you ever wondered what a heritage site would sound like? 

Step into a playful, multilingual theatre experience where heritage comes alive through unexpected voices. From the parrot perched on a gopuram to the bat in a cave temple and the elephant carved into a chariot, these characters share stories of Karnataka’s past from their own unique perspectives.

Blending humour, imagination, and history, this series of monologues offers an engaging and fresh way to encounter familiar sites—through the eyes of those who have always been there.

Performed by theatre groups Tahatto and Kahe Vidhushak.

name: Meet the Artist- afra eisma
description:
Join afra eisma for an intimate artist interaction at mild tooth of milk. This gathering offers a rare opportunity to step into eisma’s vibrant, tactile world, where themes of the body, emotion, and transformation unfold through soft sculptures and immersive forms. Expect an engaging conversation about her practice and creative process, along with a chance to connect, ask questions, and experience the atmosphere up close.

Timings

Saturday, March 28, 11:00AM- 11:30AM

Saturday, March 28, 3:00PM- 3:30PM

Sunday, March 29, 11:00AM- 11:30AM

Sunday, March 29, 3:00PM- 3:30PM

This event is held in conjunction with our exhibition, mild tooth of milk, currently on display at the Dutch Warehouse in Fort Kochi.

name: Seafoam Travelogues
description:
Image courtesy: Simar/madhumalti
Join us for an ambient musical performance with Simar/madhumalti using sounds and melodies found on coastal travels, and while surfing the internet; accompanied by visuals collected from both digital and physical worlds. 

Snippets of distant bells, crashing waves, floral poetry, login sounds, old tape recordings, and more come together to take listeners on a dreamy sound journey.

This event is held in conjunction with our exhibition, mild tooth of milk, currently on display at the Dutch Warehouse in Fort Kochi.

name: Write from Art, Write from Story
description:
Explore the layered presence of the cat as muse, metaphor, and companion through a creative writing workshop led by writer and editor Indira Chandrasekhar.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.
